biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and damp-appearing across all images, with no signs of active growth or fungal colonization.
biome_observations.debris: White fragments (likely eggshell or perlite) are visible in the P3 pot, but no organic plant life is present.
biome_observations.desk_surface: The desk surface is clean, indicating no recent soil displacement or plant debris fall.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: From the earliest image to T-3, the pots remain static and empty. From T-2 to CURRENT, the image quality degrades to total darkness, rendering further visual monitoring impossible.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: All specimens are classified as 'Dead/Lost'. There is no evidence of viable plant tissue in any of the pots provided in the sequence.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete absence of the expected flora. The transition to total darkness in the final images suggests a failure of the lighting system or camera sensor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a complete failure of the botanical biome. The pots, while present, contain no living plant material. The 'white rabbit' anchor in P3 is the only non-soil element identified. The final images are completely obscured by darkness, preventing any assessment of potential recovery or further degradation.
